extraction.one_line_summary: CATALDO Daniel won from a 200km+ breakaway after Mitchelton-Scott's repeated attacks on the final climb fractured the GC group, with NIBALI gaining time on descent.
tactical_patterns: Mitchelton-Scott attacked repeatedly on Civiglio despite Yates being down in GC, prioritizing stage hunting over defending position.
Nibali gained time on technical Como descent when Carapaz's Movistar refused to chase, exploiting descending skill differential decisively.
Roglic suffered mysterious mechanical/crash at back of bunch during attacks, revealing team positioning vulnerability when pace surges.
Movistar kept high tempo before final climb despite leading GC, inviting attacks rather than controlling defensively with numbers.

rider_signals.YATES Simon: positive: Attacked multiple times despite being 9th GC, prioritizing bonus seconds and stage result over conservation tactics.
rider_signals.NIBALI Vincenzo: positive: Bridged solo to leaders on descent after attacking Civiglio, showing late-race aggression unusual for veteran.
rider_signals.ROGLIC Primoz: negative: Lost 40+ seconds due to positioning error or mechanical during attacks, caught behind crash/problem at crucial moment.
rider_signals.CARAPAZ Richard: neutral: Movistar maintained high tempo before climbs but refused to chase Nibali's descent move, conservative maglia rosa strategy.
rider_signals.CARTHY Hugh: positive: Initiated move on Civiglio that forced reactions, EF positioning him aggressively despite modest GC position.
rider_signals.CATALDO Daniel: positive: Survived 200km+ breakaway with 15-minute max gap, holding off chasers by 20 seconds after Mitchelton brought back significantly.
